qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default-disabled authentication exposes administrative user management APIs
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets the default-auth fallback in `verifyGroups()` and the `/api/users` administrative APIs, not the advisory's unauthenticated `/api/upload` file-write handler.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Guest Socket.IO sessions can toggle device state despite secure mode
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a Socket.IO authorization bypass in `server/runtime/index.js` for `device-enable`, whereas the advisory is about the separate `/api/upload` HTTP endpoint and arbitrary file upload/write path.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated upload destination traversal permits arbitrary file overwrite
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding describes the same unauthenticated `POST /api/upload` handler referenced by the advisory in `server/api/projects/index.js`, and the same attacker-controlled file-write root cause that allows overwriting application files outside the intended upload area.

selected_finding.title: Unauthenticated upload destination traversal permits arbitrary file overwrite

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding identifies the exact advisory endpoint and vulnerable code path in `server/api/projects/index.js`, and the copied PoC proved an unauthenticated arbitrary overwrite of an application file outside the intended upload directory.
evidence: The advisory states that FUXA v1.2.7 has an unrestricted file upload in `/api/upload` that lacks authentication and can overwrite critical system files.

The advisory reference points to `server/api/projects/index.js#L193`; in v1.2.7 that handler is registered as `prjApp.post('/api/upload', ...)` without `secureFnc`, uses attacker-controlled `destination` to build `destinationDir` at line 212, and writes attacker-controlled bytes with `fs.writeFileSync()` at line 223.

The selected finding report names the same endpoint and file and explains the same root cause: `fullPath` is sanitized but the `destination` selector is trusted, so `foo/..` escapes the intended upload directory and reaches `server/main.js`.

The copied PoC executed from `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_72646eed/.judge/poc_runs/unauthenticated_upload_arbitrary_file_overwrite/` exited with code 0, and `run.log` contains `UPLOAD_POC_OK` plus `{"location":"/resources/main.js"}`, matching the verification that attacker-controlled content overwrote `server/main.js`.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC sent an unauthenticated `POST /api/upload` request and `run.log` shows `UPLOAD_POC_OK` with `{"location":"/resources/main.js"}`, proving that attacker-controlled content overwrote `server/main.js` outside the intended upload directory.
